mission.title: modelpack: Defining the diffID Generation Order in the Model Spec
mission.description: Defining the diffID Generation Order in the Model Spec. Community-requested feature.

steps.title: Check current modelpack deployment
steps.description:
Review the modelpack model-spec repository:
```bash
git clone https://github.com/modelpack/model-spec.git
cd model-spec
```
This issue is about defining the order for generating diffIDs when model layers contain directories with multiple files.

steps.title: Review modelpack configuration
steps.description:
Review how diffIDs are currently generated:

Model Spec uses files as layers, and like OCI image layers, they have a sequential relationship. The spec needs to define a fixed order for generating `diffIDs` of files within a directory to ensure consistent hashing across different implementations.

steps.title: Apply the fix for Defining the diffID Generation Order in the Model Spec
steps.description:
The fix introduces a `ModelFS` structure that defines a deterministic ordering for generating diffIDs when model layers contain multiple files in a directory.

Key changes:
- Annotations are for layer-specific descriptions
- Config is for general model properties
- `ModelFS` provides a canonical file ordering for consistent diffID generation

See the fix PR for details: https://github.com/modelpack/model-spec/pull/25

steps.title: Verify the feature works
steps.description:
Verify that diffIDs are generated consistently:
```bash
# Build a model artifact and check diffID generation
oras push <registry>/<repo>:<tag> ./model-dir:application/vnd.cnai.model.layer.v1.tar

# Verify the manifest contains correct diffIDs
oras manifest fetch <registry>/<repo>:<tag> | jq '.layers[].digest'
```
Confirm that the diffIDs are generated in a deterministic order matching the ModelFS specification.

resolution.summary: Right now we define a lot of model general information as annotations. They should be defined as a field of model config.

prerequisites.description: A working ORAS CLI installation and access to an OCI-compliant registry for testing model artifacts.
